name: Turó de l'Home Climb Loop
name_original: Vuelta a Turó de l'Home desde Plà Jua — Parque Natural del Montseny
description: This difficult 24.1-mile (38.9 km) road cycling loop to Turó de l'Home offers challenging climbs and fantastic views from the summit.
region_intro: Road cycling around Fogars De Montclús, situated within the Montseny Natural Park, offers diverse and challenging terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by demanding climbs, scenic roads, and varied forests including oak, chestnut, and beech trees. Prominent mountain peaks like Turó de l'Home and Matagalls define the landscape, providing significant elevation gain. Numerous rivers and streams also traverse the area, contributing to its natural beauty. Best road cycling routes around Fogars De Montclús The most popular road cycling route is Panoramic route of Turó l'Home – circular from Sant Celoni through the Montseny Natural Park, a 17.1 miles (27.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an ascent towards Turó de l'Home within the Montseny Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Turó de l'Home Climb Loop, a difficult 24.1 miles (38.9 km) path. This route offers a challenging climb with substantial elevation gain, leading to panoramic views from the summit. Local road cyclists also love the Santa Fe Reservoir – Mirador de Les Goitadores loop from Sant Celoni, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail leading through the Santa Fe Reservoir area, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Fogars De Montclús is defined by demanding climbs, scenic roads, and lush forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Fogars De Montclús are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 6000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fogars De Montclús's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Fogars De Montclús?
A: Road cycling in Fogars De Montclús, located within the Montseny Natural Park, is characterized by diverse and often challenging terrain. You'll encounter demanding climbs, significant elevation gains, and scenic roads winding through varied forests of oak, chestnut, and beech trees. Prominent peaks like Turó de l'Home and Matagalls define the landscape, offering substantial ascents.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, Fogars De Montclús is renowned for its challenging climbs. The ascent to &lt;strong&gt;Turó de l'Home&lt;/strong&gt; is considered one of Catalonia's 'king climbs,' offering nearly 1500 meters of elevation gain from certain approaches with steep gradients, especially near the summit. Other notable climbs include those leading to Santa Fe del Montseny and Coll Formic. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983173543/turo-de-l-home-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt; provide a difficult and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are options for various ability levels. Out of 54 available routes, 2 are classified as easy and 25 as moderate. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995566070/panoramic-route-of-turo-l-home-circular-from-sant-celoni-through-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic route of Turó l'Home – circular from Sant Celoni through the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic ascent without the extreme difficulty of the full climb.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Fogars De Montclús are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995566070/panoramic-route-of-turo-l-home-circular-from-sant-celoni-through-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic route of Turó l'Home – circular from Sant Celoni through the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26915434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Reservoir – Mirador de Les Goitadores loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes offer numerous scenic points and historical landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic views from the summit of Turó de l'Home, which can extend from the Mediterranean to the Pyrenees on clear days. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/582302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1560958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view from the Collformic road&lt;/a&gt; are also notable. Additionally, you might pass historic buildings like the church of Sant Cristòfol de Fogars de Montclús and ancient ice wells.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Fogars De Montclús?
A: The Montseny area offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. The roads, particularly on Turó de l'Home, were repaved in November 2022, improving the experience. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the lush forests suggest pleasant riding during spring and autumn. Summer can be warm, but the varied tree cover may offer some shade. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cyclists in Fogars De Montclús?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed, many routes, such as those starting from Sant Celoni, imply accessible starting points. It's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes on komoot for potential parking information or look for designated parking areas in the villages within the Montseny Natural Park.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling routes?
A: The guide mentions routes starting from Sant Celoni, which is a larger town and likely has public transport connections. For specific details on reaching Fogars De Montclús or route starting points by public transport, it's best to consult local transport schedules for buses or trains serving the Montseny Natural Park area.

Q: What natural features define the road cycling experience in this region?
A: The road cycling experience in Fogars De Montclús is defined by its stunning natural environment within the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You'll cycle through lush and varied forests, including oak, chestnut, and beech trees. Key features include the prominent mountain peaks of Turó de l'Home and Matagalls, the tranquil Santa Fe Reservoir, and numerous rivers and streams that crisscross the landscape.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: The region has charming villages and points of interest that may offer refreshment stops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Te Restaurant & Bar&lt;/a&gt; is located near a mountain pass. While specific accommodation options for cyclists aren't listed, the presence of traditional villages suggests local establishments where you might find food, drinks, or lodging. Planning your route with komoot can help identify such points.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Fogars De Montclús?
A: The road cycling routes in Fogars De Montclús are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the demanding climbs, scenic roads, and the beautiful, varied forests. The region's tranquil roads, especially on weekdays, also contribute to a highly enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Fogars De Montclús?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Fogars De Montclús. Specifically, komoot lists 54 routes, offering a wide range of options from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

The end of the paved ascent to Turó de l'Home is at Puig Sesolles, right where the military installation is located, at a height of 1,667 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered difficult and is renowned as a challenging, first-category climb in Catalonia. It holds an 'hors catégorie' (HC) rating due to its significant elevation gain (over 1250 meters) and irregular gradients. Expect sustained sections of 5-10% for nearly 6 kilometers, with some pitches exceeding 10%, and even a quarter-mile hitting 15.4%. The final 6 kilometers are particularly demanding, averaging over 8%.

faqs.question: What is the road surface like on this climb?
faqs.answer: The road surface was significantly improved with new asphalt laid in November 2022, enhancing the riding experience, especially for descents. While generally in good condition, some sections, particularly the last few hundred meters to the very peak after the car park, might still be on a narrower, potentially rougher track. Cyclists should also be cautious of tree roots that can cause the asphalt to rise in places on descents.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: Much of the climb winds through the beautiful, shady forests of the Montseny Natural Park. While these provide a pleasant natural environment, the most spectacular panoramic views are reserved for the summit. From the top, you'll be treated to fantastic views of the Mediterranean coastline, and on a clear day, parts of Barcelona can even be visible. Don't miss the View of the Vallès Oriental and Montnegre from Can Pla Lookout along the way.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike loop?
faqs.answer: The Turó de l'Home Climb Loop is approximately 38.8 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 22 minutes to complete for an experienced racebike cyclist. This duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in Montseny Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Montseny Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to check the official park website for any updated regulations or temporary restrictions, especially concerning protected areas or specific events.

faqs.question: What are the key highlights along the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the View of the Vallès Oriental and Montnegre from Can Pla Lookout, the challenging El Roc mountain pass, and the rewarding Puig Sesolles Summit, which offers stunning panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Summer can be very hot, especially on exposed sections, while winter can bring cold temperatures, ice, or even snow at higher elevations, making the climb more hazardous.

faqs.question: Is the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is not recommended for beginners. It is classified as 'difficult' and is known for its significant elevation gain and steep, irregular gradients. It requires a high level of fitness and cycling experience, making it more suitable for advanced road cyclists looking for a challenging ascent.

faqs.question: Does the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with and runs concurrently with several other official trails for significant portions. Notably, it covers 46% of the [CIMA BA04] Turó de l'Home and 33% of the [CIMA BA02] Santa Fe. You'll also find it overlapping with sections of the Sender dels Miradors, Tourdera, and the GR-5.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Montseny Natural Park generally allows dogs, this specific route is designed for racebike cycling on paved roads. Bringing a dog along for such a demanding road cycling climb is generally not practical or safe for the animal. If you plan to visit the park with a dog, consider alternative hiking trails where dogs are more easily accommodated, always keeping them on a leash as per park regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Turó de l'Home Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: While the specific starting point for this komoot tour is Pla Jua, cyclists often start the climb from various points around the Montseny Natural Park. Common starting towns with parking options include Sant Celoni or Santa Fe del Montseny. There are also smaller parking areas like 'La Carbonella' or near Fontmartina, which can serve as good access points for the climb.
